design_philosophy.core_loop: Level-based action progression with linear enemy encounters and environmental hazards
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Survival against waves of enemies with limited resources across 5 distinct levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as RoboCop, the law enforcement cyborg, eliminating criminals and corporate threats in urban environments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed property-driven design with strict adherence to Nintendo's content guidelines (evidence of censorship: removed drug/alcohol reference graphics), resulting in constrained creative vision. Game engine limitations (sprite buffer overflow, tile space exhaustion) suggest rushed or resource-constrained development that compromised intended features.

difficulty.lives_system: Multi-life system with Game Over condition
difficulty.continues_system: Password system for level progression (enables retrying specific levels)
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based checkpointing between levels; no mid-level checkpoints evident
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can select which levels to attempt via password system, allowing flexible progression order

progression.upgrade_system: Repair lab mechanic: damage assessment and repair of RoboCop's body parts (arms, legs, body) between levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Password system unlocks specific levels; progression gated by completing previous levels or entering level-specific passwords
replayability_hooks: Hi-score tables encourage score optimization across multiple playthroughs
Password system allows revisiting any completed level
Repair lab damage state persists across level selection, adding replay variation

world_building_techniques: Repair lab framing device establishing damage accumulation narrative
Licensed IP (RoboCop 3 film) provides external world context

faction_indicators: Criminal/enemy types implied by level structure
danger_signals: Level progression through increasingly hostile environments
Accumulating damage to RoboCop's body parts signaling rising threat level
environmental_storytelling: Repair lab visualizes player damage accumulation, reinforcing survival narrative
Unused level layouts suggest planned environmental variety (excised content indicates design iteration)

sources_used: tcrf - RoboCop 3 (NES) development history and technical analysis
